name: server
context: Run and configure the Dojo registry server that serves the API, search, and graph endpoints
info: The Dojo server is a Node.js Express application that loads the node tree from the filesystem, builds the search index and graph, and serves the REST API. It reads all node.json files from the nodes/ directory at startup, constructs the parent-child hierarchy, indexes aliases and triggers for search, computes backlinks for the graph, and exposes endpoints for discovery, knowledge, skills, and publishing. The server supports hot-reload in development mode — changes to node.json files are picked up without restart. Configuration is via environment variables. See [[dojo/api]] for the API contract the server implements.
body:
# Dojo Server

The server is the runtime that brings the node tree to life. It loads manifests from disk, builds indexes, and serves the API that agents query.

## Quick Start

```bash
# From the dojo repository root
cd server
npm install
npm run dev
# Server starts on http://localhost:3000
```

## Startup Sequence

1. **Load nodes** — Recursively scan `nodes/` for all `node.json` files
2. **Build tree** — Construct parent-child hierarchy from `parent` fields
3. **Index search** — Index `name`, `context`, `info`, `body`, `aliases`, `triggers`, `tags` fields
4. **Build graph** — Parse `links[]`, `related[]`, and `[[wiki-links]]` to build the edge graph
5. **Compute backlinks** — Reverse all forward edges to create backlink index
6. **Start HTTP** — Listen on `PORT` (default 3000)

## Configuration

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `PORT` | `3000` | HTTP port |
| `DOJO_NODES_DIR` | `../nodes` | Path to nodes directory |
| `DOJO_ENV` | `development` | Environment (development/production) |
| `DOJO_LOG_LEVEL` | `info` | Log level (debug/info/warn/error) |
| `DOJO_CORS_ORIGIN` | `*` | CORS allowed origins |
| `DOJO_AUTH_SECRET` | _(none)_ | JWT secret for publish endpoints |

## Development Mode

In development mode (`DOJO_ENV=development`), the server watches the `nodes/` directory for changes and hot-reloads modified nodes without restart:

```bash
DOJO_ENV=development npm run dev
# Modify any node.json → changes reflect immediately
```

## Production Mode

```bash
DOJO_ENV=production npm start
# No hot-reload, optimized for throughput
```

## Project Structure

```
server/
├── src/
│   ├── server.js        # Express app setup and startup
│   ├── loader.js        # Node tree loader from filesystem
│   ├── search.js        # Search index and query engine
│   ├── graph.js         # Graph builder and traversal
│   ├── routes/
│   │   ├── discovery.js # /v1/search, /v1/alias, /v1/tree
│   │   ├── skills.js    # /v1/skills/:uri
│   │   ├── knowledge.js # /v1/learn, /v1/graph
│   │   └── publish.js   # /v1/skills (POST)
│   └── middleware/
│       ├── auth.js      # JWT authentication
│       └── errors.js    # Error handling
├── package.json
└── tests/
```

## Health Check

```bash
curl http://localhost:3000/health
# → { "status": "ok", "nodes": 450, "uptime": 3600 }
```

## API Surface

The server implements the full [[dojo/api]] contract:
- **Discovery** — `GET /v1/search`, `GET /v1/alias/:alias`, `GET /v1/tree`
- **Skills** — `GET /v1/skills/:uri`, `GET /v1/skills/:uri/bundle`
- **Knowledge** — `GET /v1/learn/:uri`, `GET /v1/graph/:uri`
- **Publish** — `POST /v1/skills` (authenticated)

See [[dojo/api#discovery-routes]] for endpoint details.

sections.title: Quick start
sections.body: Clone the dojo repo, `cd server`, `npm install`, `npm run dev`. The server starts on `http://localhost:3000` and loads all nodes from `../nodes/`. Verify with `curl http://localhost:3000/health`. In development mode, changes to node.json files are hot-reloaded.

sections.title: Startup sequence
sections.body: The server boots in six steps: (1) load all node.json files from the nodes directory, (2) build the parent-child tree from `parent` fields, (3) index all searchable fields (name, context, info, body, aliases, triggers, tags), (4) parse links, related, and wiki-links to build the edge graph, (5) compute backlinks by reversing all forward edges, (6) start the HTTP listener. Steps 1-5 happen synchronously before the server accepts requests.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.body: All configuration is via environment variables: `PORT` (default 3000), `DOJO_NODES_DIR` (path to nodes, default ../nodes), `DOJO_ENV` (development/production), `DOJO_LOG_LEVEL` (debug/info/warn/error), `DOJO_CORS_ORIGIN` (CORS origins, default *), `DOJO_AUTH_SECRET` (JWT secret for publish endpoints). No config files — everything is environment-driven for container compatibility.

sections.title: Project structure
sections.body: The server lives in `server/src/`. Key files: `server.js` (Express setup), `loader.js` (filesystem scanner), `search.js` (index and query), `graph.js` (edge graph and traversal). Routes are split by concern: `routes/discovery.js`, `routes/skills.js`, `routes/knowledge.js`, `routes/publish.js`. Middleware handles auth and errors. Tests live in `server/tests/`.
